title: From Gas to Coal: EU Navigating Energy Security Concerns Amid ETS Constraints
section_path: From Gas to Coal: EU Navigating Energy Security Concerns Amid ETS Constraints
section_path_text: From Gas to Coal: EU Navigating Energy Security Concerns Amid ETS Constraints
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2026-05-13_from-gas-to-coal-eu-navigating-energy-security-concerns-amid-ets-constraints_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
From Gas to Coal in the EU
Amid escalating US-Iran tensions and in the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, energy markets have entered a period of heightened volatility. Given that the Middle East accounts for over 20% of global oil and gas exports, disruptions have materially impacted pricing dynamics. Notably, the combination of the Strait's closure plus Iranian attacks on Qatari LNG facilities have tightened markets and propelled global natural gas prices higher.
